item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In first paragraph of Description, something caught my eye and nagged at me. You refer to the SCP as both HE and then IT.

Other than that and a few quirks of wording, I dig it.

Dude just wants to mess with them, so they put a few mooks to work solving his weird tricks, which is in turn just them messing with him. I like. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not gonna lie, this wasn't really that enjoyable for me. Nothing in it really piqued my interest, and it didn't really go anywhere until the end. I guess you could say the twist was unexpected, but it just came out of nowhere and didn't really relate back to any other details that had already been mentioned in the article in a way that makes everything come full-circle. It's a nice concept, but the execution could be improved upon.

I think I just won't rate it for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are also a couple "foundation"s, with the lowercase F, which I consider "wrong" but might be a stylistic choice, since the ones I noticed were in conversation.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The image that is used in this article is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) and is sourced as follows:

Source: The Metropolitan Museum of Art
License: Public Domain
Title: [Man]
Author: Wilhelm von Gloeden
Release year: 1900

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Noticed a few typos in the SCP:

  • 4th paragraph - "claimed his resistance to was anomalous in nature" - might want to clarify that.
  • Incident Report 5065-1 refers to 5065 as 6065.
  • There's a missing space in Transfer Request 5065-6 - "in containing SCP-5065has been administered"

Other than that, a good read.
